summary: Phase 80 — Per-task Emergent prompt generation FULLY VERIFIED. Backend: 14/14 pytest PASS (~39s) covering generate-prompt happy path, validation (empty/short/missing text), authz (client 403, unauth 401/403), fallback code path existence, and regression on list/CRUD/doc-done/ai-docs RAG+inline_context/autonomy. Frontend Playwright: admin login → /admin/todo loaded → wand button rendered on all 31 undone todos and ZERO done todos → click wand opens todo-prompt-modal with spinner → after ~10s the LLM response renders in todo-prompt-output with all 5 Romanian sections (Obiectiv / Fișiere suspecte / Pași concreți / Criterii de validare / Risc) → Copiază button toggles label to 'Copiat!' → modal closes via X AND via backdrop click. Real Claude prompt returned (1981 chars) in proper structured Romanian. No regression detected.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_todos.py:104,125 — both endpoints use `payload: dict = Body(...)` instead of Pydantic models. Works but loses automatic 422 validation; consider TypedPromptIn(text, topic_title, priority) for consistency with TodoIn/TodoPatch.

admin_todos.py:162 — session_id is generated per-request with `uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]` (6 hex chars = 16.7M space). For an internal tool that's fine, but technically not collision-proof; using full uuid would cost nothing extra.

admin_todos.py:166-173 — fallback prompt is deterministic but never reached in observed tests (LLM always responded). The branch is correct but untested in real conditions; consider a feature flag or admin endpoint to simulate-test it, or document that it's exercised only on LLM provider outage.
admin_todos.py — no rate limit on /generate-prompt. An admin clicking the wand 100 times burns Claude tokens. Consider a per-admin cooldown (e.g., 5s between calls) or a daily budget cap.
AdminTodoBoard.jsx:97 — PromptModal renders nothing when todo is null but the parent always mounts <PromptModal/>. Fine, but a cleaner pattern is conditional `{promptTodo && <PromptModal …/>}`.

AdminTodoBoard.jsx:101-114 — copyPrompt falls back to deprecated document.execCommand('copy'); works on HTTP/insecure contexts but Chrome will eventually remove it. Current production is HTTPS so navigator.clipboard works; remove fallback to reduce dead code.
AdminTodoBoard.jsx:117 — backdrop click closes modal but also any internal click that bubbles. Inner card stops propagation correctly via onClick=stopPropagation; verified.

Romanian text in fallback uses 'verifică manual' which is correct, but a subtle UX nit: the user will see a generic prompt for both LLM-empty and LLM-error cases — both are handled the same way (setPromptError on axios error; fallback path on empty LLM response). Today errors show the red Error banner instead of the fallback prompt; behavior is correct per spec.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, wand button render on undone/hidden on done, modal open, spinner, LLM response with 5 RO sections, copy label toggle, X close, backdrop close, cleanup)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_phase80_wand_target (custom todo) and TEST_phase80_rt during testing. All cleaned up via UI delete and pytest fixture teardown. Lingering 'Test sample task' from prior iterations was not touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 80 stable. New endpoint: POST /api/admin/todos/generate-prompt {text, topic_title?, priority?} → {prompt, model, provider}. Admin-only, text<3 chars→400, empty text→400. Uses Claude via call_llm with unique session_id per request (no memory pollution). Frontend: each undone todo on /admin/todo has a Wand2 button data-testid='todo-prompt-{id}'; done todos hide it. Modal data-testids: todo-prompt-modal, todo-prompt-modal-close, todo-prompt-output, todo-prompt-copy. LLM call ~5-15s — wait up to 45s in scripts. Fallback code path exists (file-grep verified) but not exercised in live tests since LLM always responded.
